items.headline: City releases status update on youth services and library plans
items.summary: San José's City Council reviewed progress on the Children and Youth Services Master Plan and Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9th, with reports on youth programs and library improvements in development.

items.headline: City awards contract for local streets pavement repair project
items.summary: The City Council awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project on April 7th, moving forward with street infrastructure improvements across San José.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Active Transportation Plan with revised scoring
items.summary: The city council reviewed Phase 2 of the Active Transportation Plan, including edits and new scoring criteria for evaluating projects. This plan will guide how Cupertino prioritizes biking, walking, and transit improvements over the coming years.

items.headline: City Council approves construction project with new contract award
items.summary: The council adopted plans and specifications for a construction project and awarded a contract, though the agenda does not specify which project. Residents interested in local infrastructure or development should review the full meeting minutes for details on scope and location.

items.headline: Milpitas amends federal housing and community development plan
items.summary: The council considered a substantial amendment to the city's 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan, which governs how federal housing and community development funds are allocated. This could affect affordable housing initiatives and community programs in the city.

items.headline: Major senior housing complex approved near downtown
items.summary: Trillium senior apartments will bring 65 new units for seniors at 678 E St John St, with nearly $8.8M investment. This addresses San José's critical shortage of affordable senior housing options.

items.headline: Trimble Summerhill development adds 12 new homes
items.summary: Two permits issued for 6-plex buildings at Seely Avenue totaling 12 units and $4.1M in construction. Part of a larger infill development bringing medium-density housing to the area.

items.headline: City Council adopts emergency interim housing siting policy
items.summary: San José approved a new emergency interim housing siting policy on April 1st. This policy could affect where temporary housing facilities are located across the city and how they're managed.
